name: The fortifications of Tierra de Campos – circular from Ampudia
name_original: Las fortificaciones de Tierra de Campos – circular desde Ampudia
region_intro: Touring cycling around Ampudia offers routes through the expansive, undulating plains of Tierra de Campos, characterized by open vistas and low-traffic roads. The region transitions to more varied terrain, including areas near the historic Canal de Castilla. This landscape provides a mix of flat sections ideal for endurance rides and gentle rolling hills. The province of Palencia, where Ampudia is located, features diverse topography suitable for various cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Ampudia The most popular touring cycling route is The fortifications of Tierra de Campos – circular from Ampudia, a 25.3 miles (40.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete. This route explores historical defensive structures across the plains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Church of Santiago de Cigales loop from Cigales, an easy 12.7 miles (20.5 km) path. This trail offers a journey through tranquil Castilian scenery, often passing by charming villages. Local touring cyclists also love the Cuesta del Cuerno – Fuensaldaña Castle loop from Mucientes, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail leading through rolling countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 19 minutes. Touring cycling around Ampudia is defined by vast plains, historic canals, and gentle rolling hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to more demanding ascents. The routes in Ampudia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 10 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Ampudia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Ampudia?
A: There are several touring cycling routes around Ampudia, with komoot featuring &lt;em&gt;5&lt;/em&gt; distinct paths. These routes cater to various preferences, from leisurely rides to more challenging ascents across the diverse landscapes of Palencia province.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes near Ampudia?
A: The terrain around Ampudia is quite varied. You'll primarily find routes through the expansive, undulating plains of &lt;strong&gt;Tierra de Campos&lt;/strong&gt;, characterized by open vistas and low-traffic roads. Some routes also venture near the historic &lt;strong&gt;Canal de Castilla&lt;/strong&gt;, offering picturesque journeys. While generally featuring gentle rolling hills, the broader Palencia province transitions to more rugged, mountainous terrain in Montaña Palentina, ensuring a range of experiences.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes starting from Ampudia?
A: Yes, a notable circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175190519/the-fortifications-of-tierra-de-campos-circular-from-ampudia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The fortifications of Tierra de Campos – circular from Ampudia&lt;/a&gt;. This route is 25.3 miles (40.8 km) long and explores historical defensive structures across the plains, offering a moderate cycling experience.

Q: What historical landmarks can I explore on a touring bike around Ampudia?
A: Ampudia and its surroundings are rich in history. You can cycle past impressive structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5816412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Ampudia&lt;/a&gt;, a 15th-century Gothic fortress. Other nearby castles include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789312" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montealegre de Campos Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3744020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Trigueros del Valle&lt;/a&gt;. The historic Canal de Castilla also offers routes past old cereal mills and locks, blending natural beauty with industrial heritage.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easier options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33809615" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago de Cigales loop from Cigales&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.7 miles (20.5 km) path, offering a tranquil journey through Castilian scenery and charming villages. These flatter routes are ideal for those seeking a less strenuous ride.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Ampudia?
A: The region's climate makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. During these seasons, temperatures are generally mild, and the landscapes are particularly inviting. The low-traffic roads are pleasant for cycling, allowing you to fully appreciate the open vistas without extreme heat or cold.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes around Ampudia?
A: The touring cycling routes around Ampudia are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of &lt;em&gt;4.0 stars&lt;/em&gt; from 2 reviews. Cyclists often praise the quiet, well-maintained roads and the unique blend of historical sites and expansive natural landscapes found in the Tierra de Campos region.

Q: Are there any routes that offer scenic viewpoints around Ampudia?
A: Yes, several routes provide opportunities for scenic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40644628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bench at the viewpoint loop from Mucientes&lt;/a&gt; is one such route designed to lead you to picturesque spots. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3464062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Fields of Tierra de Campos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3178603" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra de Campos Viewpoint (Autilla del Pino)&lt;/a&gt; are specific highlights offering expansive vistas of the characteristic plains.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat near the cycling routes in Ampudia?
A: Ampudia and the surrounding charming villages offer various options for accommodation, cafes, and pubs. Exploring the region by bicycle allows you to stop in these villages to experience local culture and sample regional cuisine, making it easy to find places to rest and refuel during your tour.

Q: What is the typical duration for touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their length and your pace. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175190519/the-fortifications-of-tierra-de-campos-circular-from-ampudia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The fortifications of Tierra de Campos – circular from Ampudia&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 2 hours and 35 minutes, while the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31597768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuesta del Cuerno – Fuensaldaña Castle loop from Mucientes&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour and 19 minutes.

Q: Are there routes that follow the Canal de Castilla?
A: Yes, the historic Canal de Castilla is within accessible proximity to Ampudia, and cycling routes along it offer picturesque journeys. These routes provide a serene experience, passing by historical locks and tranquil Castilian scenery, perfect for those interested in both nature and heritage.

Q: Is it possible to combine cycling with visits to castles?
A: Absolutely. Many cycling routes in the Ampudia area are designed to incorporate visits to historical castles. For instance, the region is home to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5816412" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Ampudia&lt;/a&gt;, and routes can be planned to include this and other nearby fortresses like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1789313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Villalba de los Alcores&lt;/a&gt;, enriching your cycling adventure with cultural exploration.
tips: It is the most important and best preserved fortification in the province of Palencia. It was built between the 13th and 15th centuries, being declared a national monument on July 3, 1931.
Military fortress dating from the twelfth century although it was modified in the following centuries. For strategic reasons it is located on top of a hill in the town of Villalba de los Alcores.

Old inn located in the Monastery of Santa María de Matallana. Under the guest house remains of the winery and the winery are preserved. The stones from the monastery were used in the construction of the Canal de Castilla in the 18th century.
13th century fortification also known as Castillo de los Alburquerque. It is a building with square shapes and high walls located on top of a small hill in the town of Montealegre de Campos.
